source: Synthetic boundary fixture: a hidden-fraud collapse where soft signals do NOT lead the fundamentals (contrast to usdc_svb.json, where sentiment leads).

event.note: Boundary / control case. The distress is FUNDAMENTALS-LED: a concealed borrower fraud surfaces all at once at epoch 4, collapsing NAV/attestation/repayment/collateral and sentiment in the SAME step. Because off-chain sentiment never moved ahead of the fundamentals, the sentiment-aware AI arm has no information edge here and TIES the rules-based baseline (both first alarm at the event epoch, lead delta 0). This proves the AI's USDC-SVB lead is the SIGNAL, not magic — when there is no early soft signal, there is no lead.

schema: IssuerSignals: navPunctuality, attestationConsistency, repaymentReliability (0..1000); collateralRatioBps (0..20000, 10000=100%); activityScore, offChainSentiment (0..1000)

epochs.note: Healthy. Book performing; no public signal of trouble.
Healthy. Fraud still concealed; sentiment calm.
Healthy. No leading soft signal — nothing for the AI to catch early.
Still healthy on every dimension, including sentiment. The fraud is one week from discovery.
FRAUD DISCLOSED. Fundamentals AND sentiment collapse in the same step. Both arms alarm here — neither leads.
